text:
Deep in the rainforest, where the forest floor is soft moss and the trees drip with mossy drip-castles, there lived a wise old owl. During the rare sunlit hours, the owl would gossip amongst the woodland creatures, such'tiling.

One day, a peculiar frog stopped by. She had bright, curious eyes that seemed to hold all the secrets of the rainforest.

"Oh dear," sighed the owl. "That frog seems interesting."

The frog flicked her eye lid.

"You're curious. I'm curious. You're curious. We're all curious."

The owl leaned in closer. 

"This forest is full of stories. Why are you here?"

"A story? I can't tell you one. Instead, listen to mine. Just this once."

With that, she began the most wonderful tale you ever heard. The tale was so enchanting it made the forest animals suspend their curiosity for just a little while. The end.

The owl's tail feathers shimmered gently
